beats.activity: Wakes up, coffee, quick breakfast at home before heading out.
beats.reasoning: Derek's routine: up before dawn, coffee in hand. Early start is non-negotiable for a 6 AM job site.

beats.activity: Drives from Renton to Bellevue work site.
beats.reasoning: Standard commute across I-405. Clear morning, light traffic at this hour. Derek is punctual.

beats.activity: Grabs breakfast at diner, checks crew messages and site prep.
beats.reasoning: His go-to breakfast spot. Eggs, coffee, and a quick scan of overnight messages before the crew arrives.

beats.activity: Morning framing inspections and concrete work; heat building but manageable.
beats.reasoning: Core work block. Hot day means staying hydrated and adjusting crew breaks—Derek keeps pace steady. No major delays expected.

beats.activity: Lunch break: quick sandwich and hydration in shade, watching heat advisory on phone.
beats.reasoning: 91°F peak is coming soon; Derek grabs lunch on-site rather than leaving. Stays hydrated and manages crew fatigue.

beats.activity: Afternoon inspections and safety checks; wraps up early due to heat and smoke.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon heat peaks and smoke haze is noticeable; Derek cuts the day short to protect crew health. Standard practice on heat days.

beats.activity: Drives home from Bellevue to Renton.
beats.reasoning: Early departure means lighter 405 traffic. Derek heads home in time for family time before evening plans.

beats.activity: Home: quick shower, changes clothes, grabs water and a light snack.
beats.reasoning: Freshens up after a hot day. Kids are around; Derek checks in, maybe helps with something quick before heading out again.

beats.activity: Drives to Gas Works Park for the free outdoor concert.
beats.reasoning: The concert starts at 6 PM; Derek heads out early to grab a good spot and avoid the worst crowds. Clear evening, good vibe.

beats.activity: Enjoys outdoor concert at Gas Works Park; brings a beer, relaxes on the grass.
beats.reasoning: Rare evening out for Derek—clear skies, good music, and a chance to unwind. No kids, no work emails. Just a solid evening.

beats.activity: Drives back to Renton from Gas Works Park.
beats.reasoning: Late evening drive home. Traffic is light; Derek makes good time back to Renton.

beats.activity: Home: winds down, checks in with family if they're up, maybe watches a bit of sports recap.
beats.reasoning: Derek decompresses after a long day and a nice evening out. Gets ready for bed, routine wind-down.
6.5 hours of sleep before another early wake-up. Derek needs his rest for the next job site day.

diary: Started before dawn like always—coffee hit different this morning. The drive to Bellevue was smooth, grabbed my usual at Mel's and caught up on crew messages. By mid-morning the heat was really kicking in, that hazy smoke from up north making everything feel thick. We pushed through the inspections but I called it early; no point risking anyone on a day like this. Felt good to shower it all off at home, change into clean clothes. Decided to head out to Gas Works for that free concert everyone's been talking about. Sat on the grass with a cold beer watching the sun go down over the water—the smoke actually made the colors kind of beautiful, weird as that sounds. Simple evening, the kind that reminds you why you work so hard.
thought_process: I was running on routine this morning—early start, coffee, work. But around mid-afternoon something shifted. The heat and smoke were getting to me, and I realized pushing the crew hard wasn't worth it. Safety first, always. That's twenty years of construction talking. When I got home, I had this window of time and energy, and instead of collapsing on the couch, I thought about that concert. It's free, it's close enough, and honestly I needed something other than work and home. The drive across to Wallingford felt purposeful. Sitting there with the music and the view—even with the air quality warning—I felt less tired than I had all day. Sometimes you need to break the pattern, even if it's just for an evening.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
